canadian_suppliers.notes: Ontario and Quebec produce significant volumes of maple, oak, birch, and cherry. Major mill clusters in Muskoka, Haliburton, Ottawa Valley, and Laurentians.
Smaller volumes but high-quality birch, alder, and maple. Higher shipping cost to Eastern Canada.
Maple, birch, and beech available. Competitive pricing, shorter supply chains to Ontario.

international_alternatives.notes: CETA trade agreement provides zero or preferential tariff rates. Higher shipping costs but no retaliatory tariffs.

canadian_suppliers.notes: Hamilton steel corridor has multiple fastener producers. Leland Industries, Infasco are major domestic manufacturers.
Several specialty fastener shops serving furniture and construction industries.

international_alternatives.notes: Taiwan is the world's 3rd largest fastener exporter. Competitive pricing, no retaliatory tariffs apply.
Growing export capacity, lower cost base. Longer lead times (6-8 weeks).

canadian_suppliers.notes: Several domestic producers of industrial wood finishes. Saman, Sansin are notable Canadian brands.
Montreal's petrochemical cluster produces coatings and finishing chemicals.

international_alternatives.notes: High-quality finishes under CETA preferential rates. German and Italian producers well-regarded for wood finishing products.

canadian_suppliers.notes: Canada is a major global producer of pulp and paper. Cascades, Kruger Products, and Atlantic Packaging are significant domestic producers.
BC mills produce kraft paper and corrugating medium. Competitive with US Midwest mills.

international_alternatives.notes: Brazil is the world's largest eucalyptus pulp producer. Competitive pricing for kraft paper.

canadian_suppliers.notes: Alberta's petrochemical corridor produces polyethylene and polypropylene. NOVA Chemicals, Inter Pipeline are major domestic producers.
Sarnia's Chemical Valley produces base resins. Some capacity constraints on specialty grades.

international_alternatives.notes: LG Chem, Lotte Chemical are major PE/PP exporters. Competitive pricing via Pacific shipping routes.

canadian_suppliers.notes: Some domestic ink production for packaging industry. Smaller scale than US suppliers but serviceable.

canadian_suppliers.directory: Imprimeries et Industries Graphiques du Quebec
canadian_suppliers.notes: Montreal has a printing industry cluster with some ink production capability.

international_alternatives.notes: DIC Corporation (Tokyo) is the world's largest ink manufacturer. High quality, no retaliatory tariffs.

canadian_suppliers.notes: Ontario's automotive corridor has some brake component manufacturing. Smaller scale than Michigan but growing capacity.
Several national distributors source from non-US manufacturers. Can redirect supply chains to Asian or European producers.

international_alternatives.notes: Large-scale production of aftermarket brake pads and rotors. Significantly lower cost. Quality varies — require certification verification.
Premium quality under CETA preferential rates. Higher cost but OE-equivalent quality. Brembo (Italy), TRW (Germany).

canadian_suppliers.notes: Limited domestic filter manufacturing. Some repackaging and assembly operations exist.

international_alternatives.notes: Hyundai/Kia supply chain produces high-quality OE filters. Competitive pricing.
Large-scale aftermarket filter production. Lower cost, adequate quality for aftermarket applications.

canadian_suppliers.notes: Some domestic production of suspension components. Multimatic (Markham) is a notable Canadian manufacturer.

international_alternatives.notes: KYB, Tokico are major global producers. High quality, established distribution to Canada.
Bilstein, Sachs/ZF produce premium suspension. CETA preferential rates apply.

canadian_suppliers.notes: Several small-to-medium rubber products manufacturers in Southern Ontario and Quebec. Custom gasket production available.

international_alternatives.notes: Strong aftermarket gasket and seal production capability. Competitive pricing.

canadian_suppliers.notes: Some domestic packaging equipment manufacturers and parts suppliers. Limited compared to US/EU but growing.

international_alternatives.notes: World leaders in packaging machinery (Bosch, Krones). CETA preferential rates. Already used by Toronto profile for machinery.
Strong food packaging machinery sector. CETA trade agreement applies.
